name_en: Global Korea Scholarship — Undergraduate (Embassy Track)
provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education
amount: Full tuition (paid directly to university) + ₩900,000/month living allowance + round-trip economy airfare + settlement allowance ₩200,000 + departure grant ₩100,000 + health insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 4–6 years undergraduate = 5–7 years total
eligibility: Foreign nationals under 25 years old; GPA equivalent to 80% or above (or top 20% of class); citizen of a country with diplomatic relations with Korea; cannot hold Korean citizenship or permanent residency
gpa_maintenance: Undergraduate: minimum 70/100 per semester. 3 warnings = scholarship revoked (NIIED Article 18–19, effective 2025-03-01)
deadline: Embassy track: typically February–March annually (varies by country's Korean embassy)
how_to_apply: Apply through your home country's Korean Embassy. Submit documents including GKS application form, study plan, recommendation letters. Select up to 3 Korean universities (at least 1 must be Type B regional university).
tip: Embassy track is more competitive in popular countries (China, Vietnam). Type B regional universities (outside Seoul) have higher acceptance rates. Check your local Korean embassy website for country-specific deadlines — they can differ by 2–4 weeks from NIIED's general timeline.

name_en: Global Korea Scholarship — Undergraduate (University Track)
provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education
amount: Full tuition + ₩900,000/month living allowance + round-trip economy airfare + settlement allowance ₩200,000 + departure grant ₩100,000 + health insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 4–6 years undergraduate = 5–7 years total
eligibility: Foreign nationals under 25; GPA equivalent to 80% or above; recommended directly by a participating Korean university; cannot hold Korean citizenship
deadline: University track: typically March–April annually (set by each university)
how_to_apply: Apply directly to a participating Korean university. The university screens candidates and recommends them to NIIED. Contact the international office of your target university for their internal deadline.
tip: University track requires you to first research which universities accept GKS University Track nominations and contact their international office. Some universities advertise this on their admissions page; others require a direct inquiry.

name_en: Global Korea Scholarship — Graduate (Embassy Track)
provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education
amount: Full tuition + ₩1,310,000/month during degree (≈₩15,720,000/year) + ₩1,180,000/month during language training + round-trip economy airfare + settlement ₩200,000 + departure ₩100,000 + TOPIK bonus grant (TOPIK 5/6 holders) + health insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 2 years (Master's) or 3 years (PhD) = 3–4 years total
eligibility: Foreign nationals under 40; bachelor's degree holder with GPA equivalent to 80% or above; citizen of country with diplomatic relations with Korea; embassy track quota: ~800 scholars/year from 143 countries
gpa_maintenance: Graduate: minimum 80/100 per semester. 3 warnings = scholarship revoked (NIIED Article 18–19, effective 2025-03-01)
deadline: Embassy track: typically February–April annually (varies by Korean embassy in home country)
how_to_apply: Apply through your home country's Korean Embassy. Select up to 3 universities. Online application via Study in Korea website. Embassy pre-screens and submits to NIIED.
tip: TOPIK Level 5 or 6 holders receive an additional Korean Proficiency Grant on top of the regular monthly allowance. If applying from a country where Korea's embassy is in a neighboring country, check which embassy handles applications for your nationality.

name_en: Global Korea Scholarship — Graduate (University Track)
provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education
amount: Full tuition + ₩1,310,000/month during degree + ₩1,180,000/month during language training + round-trip airfare + settlement ₩200,000 + departure ₩100,000 + health insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 2 years (Master's) or 3 years (PhD)
eligibility: Foreign nationals under 40; bachelor's degree holder with GPA 80%+ equivalent; recommended by a participating Korean university; university track quota: ~1,200 scholars/year
deadline: University track: typically February–March annually (set by each university — can be earlier than embassy track)
how_to_apply: Apply to ONE university and ONE department (in-person or postal submission). University recommends you to NIIED. Cannot apply to multiple universities simultaneously on University Track.
tip: Unlike Embassy Track, University Track requires applying to only one school — choose carefully. Top universities like SNU and Yonsei have highly competitive internal GKS quotas; consider R-GKS regional track for better odds.

name_en: Global Korea Scholarship — Regional University Track (R-GKS)
provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education
amount: Same as GKS-G University Track: full tuition + ₩1,310,000/month + airfare + insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 2 years (Master's) or 3 years (PhD)
eligibility: Same as GKS-G; must apply to Type B regional universities (outside Seoul metro); 480 slots/year — significantly more quota than general university track
deadline: Follows each participating regional university's GKS University Track deadline
how_to_apply: Apply through a designated Type B regional university. Participating universities include Pusan National University (Busan), Busan University of Foreign Studies, Jeju National University, and 40+ other regional institutions.
tip: R-GKS has 480 slots vs. ~160 for general University Track — far lower competition. Regional universities in Busan, Daejeon, Gwangju, and Jeju offer high-quality programs and are more affordable cities to live in.

name_en: KOICA Scholarship Program (Korea International Cooperation Agency)
provider: Korea International Cooperation Agency (KOICA)
amount: Full tuition (paid directly to university) + ₩999,000/month living allowance + round-trip economy airfare + free university dormitory + settlement allowance (₩600,000 Master's / ₩1,200,000 PhD) + national health insurance ₩79,210/month covered
duration: 17 months (Master's) or 36 months (PhD), including Korean language training period
eligibility: Citizens of KOICA partner developing countries (40 African + 18 Latin American + 13 Asian nations including Vietnam, Indonesia, Cambodia, Philippines, Bangladesh, Nepal, Mongolia); government officials with official nomination from their home government; bachelor's degree holder; under 40 years old
deadline: Applications to KOICA regional offices or Korean Embassy typically due by July annually
how_to_apply: Must be nominated by your home government (government official status required). Submit to KOICA regional office or the Korean Embassy in your country. Not open to self-applicants.
tip: KOICA requires a government nomination — meaning applicants must be current government employees. This is NOT a scholarship for general international students. If you're from a KOICA partner country and work in a government ministry, ask your ministry's HR or international cooperation department about nomination procedures.

name_en: Seoul National University President Fellowship (SPF)
provider: Seoul National University (SNU)
amount: Full tuition (up to 6 semesters) + ₩1,500,000–₩2,000,000/month living stipend + round-trip airfare + health insurance
duration: PhD program: up to 3–4 years
eligibility: Faculty members from universities in developing countries who do not yet hold a PhD; newly admitted international PhD students with exceptional academic record; ~50 slots per year across all graduate programs
deadline: Simultaneous with SNU graduate admissions application (each semester's intake period); check en.snu.ac.kr/admission from late June (fall) or late November (spring)
how_to_apply: Apply during the SNU graduate admissions period. No separate scholarship application form — scholarship consideration is built into the PhD admissions file.
tip: The SPF targets academics from developing countries. If you're a university lecturer or researcher from Asia, Africa, or Latin America applying for an SNU PhD, this is the highest-value SNU scholarship available — highlight your university affiliation in your application.

name_en: SNU Graduate Scholarship for Excellent Foreign Students (GSFS)
provider: Seoul National University (SNU)
amount: Full tuition (max 4 semesters) + minimum ₩500,000/month (exact amount varies by department)
duration: Up to 2 years (4 semesters)
eligibility: Non-Korean international applicants accepted to participating SNU colleges/schools; eligibility verified at time of admission; not all departments participate
deadline: Simultaneous with SNU graduate admissions application
how_to_apply: Apply during SNU graduate admissions — no separate form required. Contact the specific department's international coordinator to confirm GSFS participation before applying.
tip: The monthly stipend varies widely by department (science and engineering departments tend to offer more). Confirm with your target department whether GSFS is available and what the exact monthly amount is before committing to SNU over other options.

name_en: Yonsei University Global Leader Fellowship
provider: Yonsei University
amount: Full tuition (entrance fee + tuition, up to 4 semesters; 6 semesters for joint degree) + monthly stipend (exact amount not publicly disclosed — Yonsei does not list figures on official pages; contact ysgrad@yonsei.ac.kr or 02-2123-8458 for current amount)
duration: Master's or PhD: up to 4 semesters; Joint Degree: up to 6 semesters
eligibility: Outstanding new international graduate students recommended by their college or school; GPA maintenance requirement of 3.4/4.3 or above to continue receiving scholarship
deadline: Follows Yonsei graduate admissions timeline; typically October–November for spring intake, April–May for fall intake
how_to_apply: Must be nominated by the department/college at time of admission. No standalone application — discuss scholarship potential with the department you're applying to during the admissions process.
tip: Ask your target professor about the Global Leader Fellowship before applying to Yonsei — department recommendation is required and a supporting faculty member significantly increases your chances.

name_en: Yonsei Outstanding International Student Scholarship (Type I)
provider: Yonsei University
amount: Full tuition (entrance fee + full tuition, 1 semester; renewable if GPA 3.4/4.3+ maintained)
duration: 1 semester (renewable each semester based on academic performance)
eligibility: International graduate students recommended by their department; minimum GPA 3.4/4.3 for renewal
deadline: Each semester, per Yonsei's academic calendar (typically August for fall, February for spring)
how_to_apply: Department recommendation required. Existing students apply through their department coordinator; renewal requires GPA documentation.
tip: Unlike the Global Leader Fellowship, this scholarship can also be awarded to continuing students (not just incoming). If your GPA drops below 3.4/4.3 in any semester, you lose eligibility for renewal.

name_en: Korea University GSIS International Student Scholarship — Type A
provider: Korea University, Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS)
amount: 100% tuition + ₩500,000/month for 4 months per semester (= ₩2,000,000/semester living stipend)
duration: Duration of GSIS program; renewable based on academic performance
eligibility: International graduate students admitted to KU GSIS; TOEFL iBT 90 / IELTS 7.0 / TOPIK Level 6 required at time of application; scholarship assigned at admission without separate application
deadline: Follows KU GSIS admissions deadline: typically around October for spring intake, April for fall intake
how_to_apply: Apply to KU GSIS through the regular admissions process. Scholarship type is automatically assigned based on your language test scores and academic profile — no separate scholarship form needed.
tip: Type A requires TOPIK 6 or TOEFL 90+. If your score is TOPIK 4 or TOEFL 82, you'll be assigned Type B (60% tuition, no monthly stipend). Retaking TOPIK before applying to push from Level 4 to Level 6 is worth the effort for the ₩2M/semester cash difference.

name_en: Korea University GSIS International Student Scholarship — Type B
provider: Korea University, Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS)
amount: 60% tuition coverage (humanities track); no monthly living stipend
duration: Duration of GSIS program; renewable based on performance
eligibility: International graduate students admitted to KU GSIS humanities track; TOEFL iBT 82 / IELTS 6.0 / TOPIK Level 4 required
deadline: Follows KU GSIS admissions deadline
how_to_apply: Automatically assigned during KU GSIS admissions — no separate application required.
tip: Type B covers 60% of tuition with no monthly allowance — you'll need to budget for the remaining 40% tuition plus all living costs. Combining with a part-time job (permitted up to 20 hours/week on D-2) is a common approach.

name_en: KAIST International Student Graduate Fellowship
provider: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
amount: Full tuition waiver (automatic) + ₩1,000,000/month (Master's) or ₩300,000/month base (PhD, supplemented by research stipend from supervisor) — College of Engineering PhD Fellowship: ₩2,500,000/month
duration: Duration of graduate program (2 years Master's, 3–4 years PhD)
eligibility: All international students admitted to KAIST graduate programs; dual Korean citizens excluded; TOEFL iBT 83 or IELTS 6.5 required for admission
deadline: KAIST admissions: August 18 – September 1 (for spring intake); February window (for fall intake)
how_to_apply: Auto-awarded with admission — no separate scholarship application. Apply to KAIST graduate admissions at kaist.edu/en/admissions; scholarship is part of the admission package.
tip: KAIST is the only major Korean university where ALL admitted international graduate students receive a full tuition waiver automatically. PhD students typically receive additional research stipend from their lab supervisor, making total monthly income significantly higher than the base ₩300,000. Ask about your potential supervisor's lab funding during interviews.

name_en: SKKU Global STEM Scholarship
provider: Sungkyunkwan University (SKKU)
amount: Up to 100% tuition + ₩600,000 one-time settlement fund (first semester only); merit-based partial scholarship available from second semester onward based on GPA
duration: First semester auto-awarded; continuation each semester based on GPA
eligibility: International students admitted to SKKU natural science, engineering, or medical degree programs; auto-awarded with admission (no separate application)
deadline: Follows SKKU admissions deadline (typically September–October for spring, March–April for fall)
how_to_apply: Automatically awarded to all SKKU STEM admits. For non-STEM (humanities/social sciences), the Admission Scholarship covers 10–100% of first-semester tuition based on academic profile.
tip: SKKU STEM programs pair well with Samsung-affiliated research opportunities — ask your admissions counselor about industry internship connections during the scholarship interview process.

name_en: R-GKS via Pusan National University (Regional Track)
provider: Pusan National University (on behalf of NIIED/MOE)
amount: Same as GKS-G: full tuition + ₩1,310,000/month + airfare + health insurance
duration: 1 year Korean language training + 2 years (Master's) or 3 years (PhD)
eligibility: Same as GKS-G general requirements; must apply to Pusan National University specifically through this track; 480 total R-GKS slots across all participating regional universities
deadline: Typically February–March annually; contact PNU international office for exact date
how_to_apply: Apply through Pusan National University's GKS University Track (R-GKS). Visit the PNU Office of International Affairs or email directly to obtain application package.
tip: Busan is South Korea's second-largest city with significantly lower cost of living than Seoul, making the ₩1,310,000/month stipend stretch further. PNU is particularly strong in engineering, business, and life sciences.

name_en: Jeju National University International Student Mileage Scholarship
name_ko: 제주대학교 외국인 유학생 비교과 마일리지 장학금
provider: Jeju National University
amount: Partial tuition scholarship (amount varies based on accumulated mileage points); not a full scholarship
duration: Each academic year; based on participation in university programs throughout the year
eligibility: International students enrolled at Jeju National University; earn mileage points by participating in programs run by the International Student Support Center (language exchanges, cultural events, mentoring, etc.)
deadline: Applications for mileage scholarship typically at end of each semester; mileage accumulated throughout the year
how_to_apply: Enroll in eligible non-curricular programs through the Jeju National University International Exchange Division (국제교류처, Ara Muse Hall 3F). Mileage is tracked automatically; apply for scholarship conversion at semester end.
tip: This scholarship rewards active participation over academic scores — ideal for students who engage with campus life. Jeju National University is also one of the R-GKS participating institutions, so high-need students can pursue the R-GKS for full funding instead.

name_en: Seoul Tech Scholarship
provider: Seoul Future Foundation (서울미래인재재단 / seoulfuture.or.kr)
amount: Full tuition (tuition + registration fee) + living allowance + health insurance + one-way airfare; estimated total ~₩20,000,000/year
duration: Up to 2 years (Master's program)
eligibility: Non-Korean nationals (applicant AND both parents must not hold Korean citizenship); under 40 years old; newly admitted or enrolled in a STEM Master's program at a designated Seoul university; ~30 slots per year (max 5 per country)
deadline: Online application: December 22 – March 6 (16:00 KST); postal documents: by March 25; final results announced July. Next cycle opens December 2026.
how_to_apply: Submit application via Google Forms + postal documents to Seoul Future Foundation. Email: sts@seoulfuture.or.kr
tip: Only 30 slots with a max of 5 per country — competition is low for nationalities with fewer applicants. Seoul Future Foundation's other scholarships (서울희망 대학진로 등) are Korean nationals only; Seoul Tech Scholarship is the sole program open to international students.

name_en: Busan Activity-Based Scholarship for International Students
provider: Busan Global City Foundation (부산글로벌도시재단 / bgcf.or.kr), commissioned by Busan Metropolitan City
amount: Monthly activity-based points converted to scholarship; exact amount not publicly disclosed (contact foundation directly)
duration: 1 academic year; up to 18 recipients per cycle
eligibility: International students enrolled in a regular degree program (undergraduate or graduate) at a Busan university; must participate in community service, foundation events, and civic exchange activities as part of the scholarship
deadline: Annual recruitment: late March – early April (2024 cycle confirmed; 2025 status unconfirmed — call 051-711-6862 to verify)
how_to_apply: Submit application form + personal statement with advisor signature via email to osj221@bfic.kr. Contact: 부산글로벌도시재단 세계시민협력팀 051-711-6862
tip: This is an activity-participation scholarship, not a tuition waiver — you must commit time to foundation programs throughout the year. Only 18 slots city-wide. Better financial coverage is available through R-GKS at Pusan National University or Busan University of Foreign Studies.

name_en: National Health Insurance (NHIS) — International Student Rate
provider: National Health Insurance Service (NHIS / 건강보험공단)
amount: ₩79,320/month (2026 rate) for D-2 students — 50% discounted rate; standard adult rate would be ₩158,000+. D-2 students: 50% discount applied automatically. D-4 students: enrolled 6 months after entry.
duration: Continuous while enrolled as an international student in Korea
eligibility: D-2 visa holders: automatic enrollment upon alien registration. D-4 visa holders: automatic enrollment 6 months after entry. Must pay monthly or visa extension may be denied.
deadline: Monthly premium due by the 25th of each month; enrollment is automatic (no application needed)
how_to_apply: No application required. NHIS automatically enrolls D-2/D-4 students after alien registration. Premium billed to your Korean address; pay at any bank, convenience store, or via mobile banking app.
tip: Missing even one NHIS payment can result in denial of visa extension — Korean immigration checks payment history. Set up automatic bank transfer (자동이체) at your Korean bank as soon as you open an account. GKS scholars have NHIS paid by the scholarship; check with your university if you're unsure.

name_en: University Language Institute Scholarships (D-4 Visa)
provider: Various Korean universities (language institutes)
amount: Typically 10–50% tuition waiver; varies by institution and TOPIK level achieved; some institutes offer full waivers for top-performing students
duration: Each term (usually 10-week terms); renewable based on attendance and TOPIK progress
eligibility: Enrolled D-4 students at the university's language institute; scholarship criteria usually based on attendance rate (90%+ required), TOPIK exam results, or general academic performance in language classes
deadline: Each term enrollment period; varies by university
how_to_apply: Apply through your language institute's student affairs office at the beginning or end of each term. Some scholarships are auto-awarded based on attendance; others require a written application. Ask during enrollment.
tip: D-4 students cannot receive GKS or most government scholarships. Language institute scholarships are the primary financial support available. Taking and passing TOPIK while at the institute often unlocks higher scholarship tiers — ask your institute coordinator which TOPIK level triggers a scholarship increase.
